records.ety: [F. monde the world, L. mundus. See Mundane.]

defs: A ball or globe forming part of the regalia of an emperor or other sovereign. It is encircled with bands, enriched with precious stones, and surmounted with a cross; -- called also globe.

records.ety: [OE. mound, mund, protection, AS. mund protection, hand; akin to OHG. munt, Icel. mund hand, and prob. to L. manus. See Manual.]

defs: An artificial hill or elevation of earth; a raised bank; an embarkment thrown up for defense; a bulwark; a rampart; also, a natural elevation appearing as if thrown up artificially; a regular and isolated hill, hillock, or knoll.
quotes: To thrid the thickets or to leap the mounds.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mounded; p. pr. & vb. n. Mounding.]

defs: To fortify or inclose with a mound.
